reasoning: Polymarket acquired a CFTC-licensed Designated Contract Market (DCM), QCX LLC, for $112 million and received a no-action letter resolving previous regulatory issues with the CFTC. They filed formal self-certifications for their first event contracts, initially targeting an early October 2025 launch. On October 7th, ICE (owner of the NYSE) announced a $2 billion investment in Polymarket, signaling massive institutional confidence in the platform's US comeback. While a temporary U.S. government shutdown delayed the CFTC from processing the paperwork (which only requires one business day of no objections to take effect), there are still over two months remaining in the year for the shutdown to resolve and the platform to go live. An internal market on Polymarket forecasting this exact outcome was trading at 99% 'Yes' in October. Given the overwhelming financial backing, successful regulatory maneuvering, formal filings already submitted, and crowd consensus, it is highly probable that the platform will facilitate a real-money trade by December 31, 2025.
